channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Chronic insomnia: A personal struggle and coping strategies
item.description: The article explores the author's experience with chronic insomnia, a condition affecting at least 60% of the population. It details the challenges of falling asleep, the impact on daily life, and var...

item.title: Uhuru Kenyatta Commemorates Former IEBC Chair Abdulahi Sharawe
item.description: Former President Uhuru Kenyatta has expressed deep sorrow following the passing of Engineer Abdulahi Sharawe, a key figure in Kenya's public service who chaired the Independent Electoral and Boundarie...

item.title: Yas invests Sh1.2 trillion in tech to boost Tanzania's digital healthcare
item.description: Yas Tanzania has committed Sh1.2 trillion over four years to expand 4G/5G networks, aiming to transform Tanzania's healthcare system through digital solutions. The investment supports telemedicine, di...

item.title: Impeachment Court Grants Subpoenas for Sara Duterte's Financial Records, Defense Seeks Legal Remedies
item.description: The defense team of Vice President Sara Duterte submitted to the Senate impeachment court's ruling granting subpoenas for her financial records but emphasized they may pursue legal remedies. The court...

item.title: Iran's Interior Minister Momeni arrives in Pakistan amid regional tensions
item.description: Iran’s Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni arrived in Islamabad on an official visit, meeting Pakistani officials including Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, and Chief of De...

item.title: NPA forfeits R326m assets linked to Tembisa Hospital corruption scandal
item.description: The National Prosecuting Authority (NPA) has initiated the seizure of R326m in assets belonging to Hangwani Maumela, a key figure implicated in the Tembisa Hospital looting scandal. The Johannesburg H...

item.title: Meta to face Tennessee trial over allegations Instagram's design harmed young users
item.description: Meta Platforms is due to stand trial in Tennessee over allegations that Instagram was deliberately designed to encourage compulsive use among children and teenagers, contributing to mental health prob...

item.title: TV Series Adaptation of Supernatural Novel by Mike Mignola and Thomas Sniegoski in Development
item.description: The supernatural thriller podcast 'Grim Death and Bill the Electrocuted Criminal' is being adapted into a TV series by Echoverse, a sci-fi and fantasy audio content studio. The project is based on a n...

item.title: Young Women Share Experiences at CJP Protest, Reflect on Challenges Faced
item.description: The article highlights the experiences of young women participating in a protest organized by the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP). These women recounted how they were lied to by authorities, faced police ...

item.title: Alberta Municipalities Banned from Campaigning on Separatism Referendum
item.description: As Alberta’s fall referendum on separatism approaches, municipalities are barred from campaigning for or against the issue. Elections Alberta rules prohibit mayors and councillors from raising funds o...

item.title: Kenya Govt and Tourism Federation Condemn Gachagua's 'Avoid Kenya' Remarks
item.description: The Kenya State Department for Tourism, through Principal Secretary Julius Bitok, has strongly condemned remarks by impeached deputy president Rigathi Gachagua urging foreign tourists and investors to...

item.title: Ontario MPP Stan Cho Faces Scandal Over Taxpayer-Funded Expenses
item.description: Ontario Progressive Conservative Party MPP Stan Cho has faced renewed scrutiny over his riding association expenses, which include thousands of dollars in meals, hotel stays, and luxury travel billed ...

item.title: Protests in Delhi Demand Statehood for Occupied Kashmir Amid Political Tensions
item.description: Hundreds of people, led by the chief minister of India-occupied Kashmir (IoK), staged a protest in Delhi demanding statehood for the region. The demonstration, which took place seven years after the a...

item.title: Akbayan Condemns China's Attack on Philippine Navy in Ayungin Shoal
item.description: Akbayan, a political party in the Philippines, has strongly condemned what it described as a 'barbaric' and deliberate attack by the China Coast Guard (CCG) on Philippine Navy personnel in Ayungin Sho...

item.title: Iran retaliates against US strikes, fuel prices rise to $4 a gallon as conflict escalates
item.description: A cycle of escalating violence between Iran and the US has resulted in at least three American service members killed since hostilities resumed a week ago. Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IR...

item.title: Open AI Models: A Form of Healthy Competition, Not a Security Threat
item.description: The article discusses the inevitability of open AI models and argues against treating them as security threats. It highlights how companies like Anthropic and OpenAI, valued near $1 trillion, face com...

item.title: Southern California Faces Dual Threat of Heatwave and Tropical Storm Elida
item.description: Southern California is bracing for a dangerous heatwave that will last seven days, with temperatures potentially exceeding 100°F (38°C) in many areas. The National Weather Service has issued warnings ...

item.title: Andy Burnham officially becomes UK Prime Minister after Keir Starmer's resignation
item.description: Andy Burnham has been officially installed as the UK Prime Minister by King Charles III following Keir Starmer's resignation after two years in office. The Labour leader, who orchestrated a leadership...

item.title: Marathi Web Series Explores Mom vs Mon-in-Law Dynamics
item.description: The trailer of the Marathi web series 'Aga Aai Aaho Aai' highlights a compelling family drama centered around the conflict between a mother-in-law and daughter-in-law. Directed by Varun Narvekar and w...

item.title: Twin Sisters Pursue Occupational Therapy to Help Family Member with Cerebral Palsy
item.description: Sheryl Chepkirui and Beryl Chepkogei, twin sisters from Uasin Gishu County, enrolled at Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) Mbuvo Campus for the March 2025 intake. Their decision to study Occupation...
